title: Crayon publication routes (.crayon/publish.yaml)
description: Low-code materialization of `Sink` instances run by `crayon publish` — the symmetric 'write this there' mirror of the rule engine. Each route selects a concrete sink `kind` (a dependency-free starter kind, or a custom subclass in `.crayon/sinks/`), names the source content (a file or file/section), and supplies endpoint parameters validated by the sink class. Lives in the repo so routes are versioned and reviewable.

routes.description: Ordered list of executable publication routes.

route.description: One executable route: a concrete sink `kind`, a `source`, plus endpoint parameters. Additional kind-specific keys are permitted and validated by the sink class.

kind.description: Registry key of the concrete `Sink` subclass (e.g. `file`, `http_post`, or a custom kind from `.crayon/sinks/`).

source.description: The content routed out: a whole file, or a heading-bounded section of a file.

file.description: Repo-relative POSIX path of the `.md` file.

heading.description: When present, routes only the section under this heading (a file/section source); otherwise the whole file.
